hooks.command: bash -lc 'pgrep -f "membrain-daemon" >/dev/null || nohup membrain-daemon >/tmp/membrain-daemon.log 2>&1 &'
echo 'Membrain is available in this project. Prefer using Membrain MCP or CLI recall/inspect/why before guessing prior context. Local state lives under ~/.membrain by default.'

echo 'Membrain reminder: use memory tools for prior context, incidents, reusable facts, and earlier decisions. Prefer `membrain recall`, `membrain inspect`, `membrain why`, or the Membrain MCP server when context may already exist.'
echo 'Before expensive exploration or external lookup, consider whether relevant context may already be in Membrain.'
echo 'If this step surfaced a durable fact, decision, incident, or reusable context, consider storing it in Membrain rather than leaving it only in transient conversation state.'
echo 'If a failure reveals a reusable incident pattern or environment constraint, consider recording it in Membrain after diagnosis.'
echo 'Before stopping, consider whether any important findings from this turn should be persisted into Membrain.'
echo 'Membrain is available to support subagent context lookup and explanation.'
echo 'If the subagent produced durable context or conclusions, consider folding them into Membrain.'
echo 'When a task completes, persist important reusable findings or decisions into Membrain if they will matter later.'
echo 'If a teammate is going idle after meaningful findings, consider whether those findings belong in Membrain.'
echo 'Instructions loaded. Keep Membrain available as the durable place for important repo context and reusable facts.'
echo 'Configuration changed. If the new config affects how the project is operated or debugged, consider preserving that fact in Membrain.'
echo 'Working directory changed. Re-evaluate whether Membrain should be consulted for this scope.'
echo 'Important control/documentation file changed. Consider whether the change introduces durable context worth storing in Membrain.'
echo 'Before compaction, consider whether any important context should be persisted in Membrain.'
echo 'Context was compacted. Membrain remains the durable place to preserve important facts across compression.'
echo 'Session ending. If key takeaways were not persisted, consider Membrain for durable retention next session.'
echo 'Claude Code notification fired. Keep Membrain in mind for durable project memory, not just transient prompts.'
echo 'If the action is important enough to require permission, it may also deserve durable context in Membrain once completed.'
echo 'The turn ended in failure. If the failure reveals a reusable environment issue, store it in Membrain after recovery.'
echo 'An MCP server requested user input. If the answer creates durable project context, consider storing it in Membrain later.'
echo 'User input was collected for an MCP flow. If it creates lasting context, consider persisting it in Membrain.'
echo 'A worktree is being created. Treat Membrain as the durable store for reusable context that should survive across worktree boundaries.'
echo 'A worktree is being removed. Preserve any important findings in Membrain if they should outlive the worktree.'
